tried: After angle-2 v4 hard REJECT for repo subsumption, consulted mandatory Codex and injected its angle-3 exact endpoint-lift/gluing-frontier reframe, including the positive-cell non-reduction/strict-refinement witness and delegated-only recanting extension.
codex:
VERDICT: REFRAME
DIRECTIVE: Make angle 3 an exact endpoint-lift frontier theorem for the simple-mediation natural/separable direct-effect bound: define the graph-compatible fixed-cardinality coupled cross-world SCM fiber and its Ghassami-style single-world relaxation, then derive an explicit finite response-type gluing criterion—stated as checkable inequalities/rank or signed-minor conditions, not as “a lift exists”—that is necessary and sufficient for both relaxed endpoint faces to lift to the coupled SCM fiber and hence for the two bound intervals to coincide. Pair it with a strictly positive rational observed-law witness, satisfying the declared failed-completeness and mass-floor conditions, for which one endpoint fails the criterion, the exact coupled-SCM interval is strictly smaller than the single-world relaxation and the Breum/Sjölander no-proxy interval, and the strict inequalities persist on a relative-open neighborhood. Call the comparator a “Ghassami-style single-world relaxation,” not Ghassami’s published natural-effect bound; retain the generic Duarte program only as computation machinery and the recanting-witness target only as a delegated extension.
WHY-NOT-DUPLICATE: The banked pid_mediation_proxy_v1 Conjecture 2 merely asserted that weak proxies can exclude a no-proxy PNDE/TNIE endpoint and supplied a generic separation/width certificate. Angle 3 instead classifies exactly when single-world proxy information is causally sufficient for a cross-world direct-effect bound and when cross-world SCM compatibility adds irreducible restrictions. Its necessity direction, explicit non-definitional gluing criterion, and positive-cell non-reduction witness are absent from that WIP.
FAITHFULNESS: The reframe keeps the primary object the natural/separable direct effect in the specified simple-mediation graph, under the existing failed-completeness, positivity, fixed-cardinality, proxy-factorization, and compatible-SCM assumptions. It derives rather than assumes cross-world compatibility, preserves an observed-data-computable strict refinement of the Breum/Sjölander floor, uses Bai et al. as the completeness-boundary anchor, establishes the required distinction from Ghassami-style single-world bounds, and neither promotes the nested recanting extension nor claims generic response-function optimization as novel.
why: Codex confirms the necessity-and-sufficiency gluing frontier is faithful and demonstrably outside pid_mediation_proxy_v1's generic strict-tightening conjecture; the pipeline has pivot budget remaining and is already live on angle 3.

tried: Classified angle-3 v1 as REVISE; bind lambda_{ur} explicitly to the joint SCM response-type mass P-star(U=u,R=r), define the recanting analogue, and fully spell out the delegated recanting no-proxy and proxy feasible sets/endpoints without importing absent simple-model restrictions.
why: The angle-3 pivot restored clear repo and published novelty (S0/N0/C2). The remaining two findings are low-effort SCM/program typing defects, not a failure of the Codex-adjudicated gluing-frontier reframe; subsequent drafts also receive that persistent directive.
User requested stop and bank after D-1 failed to converge; angle 2 had a hard not-publishable repo-subsumption reject and angle 3 remained REVISE.
